data.title: Trader Eugene posted an article "Surrendering to the Market": Licking his wounds and waiting to see what happens next.
data.description: According to @ai_9684xtpa, trader Eugene posted on his personal channel, "I acted too early; the current market is hellishly difficult. I'm licking my wounds and will observe again." This seems to be an admission of a previous trading decision error. Previously, he had publicly stated that he had increased his holdings of ETH and SOL and was bullish on UNI in the short term. Now that UNI has fallen below the $6.5 support level, his previous arguments have become invalid.

data.title: Investor Reconsiders Strategy Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description: According to Foresight News, a user known as @ai_9684xtpa has reported that an investor, referred to as 'Pigeon' in a Telegram group, admitted to acting prematurely in the market. The investor hinted at reassessing their position, suggesting they might be dealing with losses or holding onto investments. Previously, Pigeon had publicly announced an increase in holdings of ETH and SOL, expressing short-term optimism for UNI. However, UNI has since fallen below the $6.5 threshold, undermining their earlier market predictions.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Acquires Significant Holdings Amid Debt Concerns
data.description: According to Foresight News, blockchain monitoring service Lookonchain has reported that a major Ethereum investor, known as 66kETHBorrow, has purchased 114,684 ETH over the past two days, valued at approximately $319 million. This acquisition brings the investor's total holdings to 489,696 ETH, worth around $1.35 billion. The address is currently facing a debt of $648 million on the Aave platform, with an ETH liquidation price set at approximately $1,594.

data.title: Nakamoto CEO: MSCI's removal of digital asset reserve companies highlights industry discrimination and conflicts of interest; regulatory agencies should intervene and investigate.
data.description: Regarding the recent market rumors of MSCI removing digital asset companies from its index, Nakamoto CEO and Bitcoin Magazine Chairman David Bailey posted on the X platform, stating, "For a systemically important stock index, after years of actively supporting the inclusion of Bitcoin and digital assets, to single out Bitcoin and digital asset companies for de-indexing and disqualification is discriminatory and capricious. We should call it Operation Choke Point 3.0. What has changed? Why is Bitcoin being singled out while all other commodity-related industries (oil, gold, agriculture) are fully eligible for indexing? If this policy passes, it will: • Harm investors by triggering a forced sell-off of hundreds of billions of dollars in Bitcoin stocks and hundreds of billions of dollars in lost future earnings. • Cool investment in Bitcoin startups and private companies that rely on public market exits. • Prevent publicly traded companies from holding or using Bitcoin due to concerns about index eligibility. • Weaken America's leadership in a strategically important, rapidly growing global industry. I believe this policy topic was first published on October 10th." The current situation is highly questionable, and I believe it is likely to be a significant factor in our future market conditions. Banks and index providers should not have the right to exclude 40% of capital market funding from the entire industry, especially when there is direct competition within the industry. This is a clear conflict of interest and absolutely deserves scrutiny from Congress and regulators. We fought and won fair access for banks. We fought and won against the Bitcoin ETF. Now we must fight (and win) for the public's right to fair investment.

data.title: Moonrock Capital founder alleges insider trading allegations surrounding Coinbase's acquisition of Vector.fun; TNSR stock price surges 8-fold in two days before plummeting 40%.
data.description: Simon Dedic, founder of Moonrock Capital, posted on X that the Coinbase acquisition of Vector.fun showed signs of insider manipulation. He noted that TNSR surged approximately eightfold in two days while the overall market was weak, only to fall back about 40% on the day the news was announced, with no apparent attempts to conceal the activity by the relevant accounts. He believes that Vector.fun's developer, Tensor, and its token appear to remain independent of the acquisition, and infers that Tensor will cash out a large, undisclosed sum, while TNSR holders will "get nothing," further highlighting the misalignment between equity and tokens.

data.title: How AI Broke the Four-Year Crypto Cycle — and Why 2026 Still Matters
data.description:
Key Insights AI changed the usual mechanics of the four-year crypto cycle, and that shift helps explain why the expected rate-cut-driven crypto boom did not arrive in 2025, even as institutions flowed capital into digital assets. The observation, first set out in a widely circulated thread by X user Gammichan on Nov.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/11/21/how-ai-broke-the-four-year-crypto-cycle-and-why-2026-still-matters/

data.title: Strategy stated that they persisted in buying BTC even when it fell to 50% of their purchase price during the last bear market, suggesting they will continue to increase their holdings.
data.description: Bitcoin treasury firm Strategy posted on the X platform that during the 2022 crypto bear market, the company's average purchase cost was $30,000, while the trading price of Bitcoin once fell to only $16,000, with the market price only 50% of its purchase cost. However, Strategy bought more Bitcoin, suggesting that it will continue to increase its Bitcoin holdings during this bear market.

data.title: Solana Spot ETFs Experience Significant Inflows on November 21
data.description: According to Odaily, data from SoSoValue indicates that on November 21, Eastern Time, Solana spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $10.58 million. The 21Shares SOL ETF TSOL recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $5.97 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $7.17 million. Fidelity SOL ETF FSOL followed with a single-day net inflow of $2.97 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $12.81 million. As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETFs stands at $719 million, with a Solana net asset ratio of 1.01%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $510 million.

data.title: The US Solana spot ETF saw a net inflow of $10.58 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the Solana spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $10.58 million yesterday (November 21, Eastern Time). The SOL spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the 21Shares SOL ETF (TSOL), with a net inflow of $5.97 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $7.17 million. This was followed by the Fidelity SOL ETF (FSOL), with a net inflow of $2.97 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $12.81 million. As of press time, the Solana spot ETF has a total net asset value of $719 million, a net asset value ratio of 1.01%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $510 million.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $11.89 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (November 21st, Eastern Time), the XRP spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $11.89 million. The Bitwise XRP ETF saw a single-day net inflow of $11.28 million, with a historical total net inflow of $117 million. The Canary XRPC ETF saw a single-day net inflow of $620,000, with a historical total net inflow of $306 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF is $384 million, the XRP net asset ratio is 0.33%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $423 million.

data.title: A month ago, a whale that had been holding ASTER tokens sold off its entire position for $645,000 worth of tokens, making a meager profit.
data.description: According to onchainschoo.pro, a whale that had been holding ASTER tokens and profiting from them liquidated all the tokens it bought a month ago, selling $645,000 worth of tokens in the past 6 hours, resulting in a meager profit. The wallet address is 0x94a19f9251dd7183dadeda6cbdb3802364d0bb8e.

data.title: Billionaire Bill Ackman: Crypto market sell-off leads to decline in US mortgage lender stocks; short-term panic impacts US stock performance.
data.description: Billionaire and renowned hedge fund manager Bill Ackman posted on the X platform, stating, "I underestimated Fannie and Freddie's (F2, the major U.S. mortgage lender) exposure to cryptocurrencies, not on the balance sheet, but on a shareholder basis. Think about the correlation between F2 and Bitcoin, and you'll understand what I mean. Forced liquidations and margin calls in the cryptocurrency market are driving the sale of F2 stock in the market. We don't own Bitcoin, but clearly in the short term, we have a proxy in the Bitcoin stock market. In the short term, technical factors may outweigh fundamentals. F2 is a case in point."

data.title: Crypto Market Remains on Edge After Over $2B Wiped Out in the Last 24 Hours
data.description:
The crypto market is reeling once again after an intense wave of liquidations erased over $2 billion in leveraged positions within a single day. Related Reading: Ethereum Dead Cat Bounce Puts Price At $3,400, But What’s The Ultimate Target? With Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, and other major assets plunging sharply, traders are bracing for what could be another turbulent stretch, especially with billions in options set to expire. ETH's price trends to the downside on the daily chart
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/crypto-market-remains-on-edge-after-over-2b-wiped-out-in-the-last-24-hours/

data.title: Machi's Hyperliquid account balance shrank to approximately $7,200, and his 25x leveraged long ETH position had a floating profit of less than $1,000.
data.description: According to Hyperbot website data, after multiple liquidations, Machi Big Brother's Hyperliquid account assets are reduced to only $7,282.32. He currently holds a 25x long position in 56.32 ETH, with a position value of only $156,000, currently showing a floating profit of approximately $600. The liquidation price was $2,695.93.

data.title: Coinbase CEO: The acquisition of Vector.fun marks the beginning of introducing native Solana technology and talent, which will enhance Coinbase's support for Solana transactions.
data.description: Regarding the recent acquisition of Vector.fun by Coinbase, Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong posted on the X platform, stating, "Earlier this year, I said we would improve our Solana gaming capabilities. Since then, we've introduced AgentKit and x402 to Solana and upgraded our infrastructure to expand the service (5x increase in block throughput and 4x increase in RPC performance). Now, we're bringing Solana's native technology and talent to our platform to enhance Coinbase's support for Solana transactions. This is how we build the exchange for everything."

data.title: US real estate investment firm Cardone Capital increased its holdings by 185 BTC at an average price of $82,500 per BTC, worth over $15 million.
data.description: Grant Cardone, founder of the US real estate investment firm Cardone Capital, posted on the X platform that Cardone Capital increased its holdings by 185 bitcoins, worth approximately $15.26 million, when bitcoin was at $82,500.

data.title: A whale used 20x leverage to go long on BTC, with an average opening price of $84,400.4.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet deposited $1.7 million into HyperLiquid to open a 20x leveraged long position in BTC, with an average opening price of $84,400.4 and a position size of 104.36 Bitcoins. In addition, this whale made a profit of $705,000 in another wallet.

data.title: Bitcoin's price has plummeted by as much as 25% in the past 10 days, and the hashtag "Bitcoin crashes, more than 400,000 people liquidated" has topped the trending topics list on Douyin (TikTok).
data.description: Related data shows that the hashtag "Bitcoin crashes, over 400,000 liquidated" topped the Douyin (TikTok) trending list. OKX data shows that Bitcoin fell from a high of 107,500 USDT on November 11th to a low of 80,636 USDT on November 21st, a drop of up to 25% in the past 10 days. As of November 22nd, nearly $1.81 billion in liquidations occurred across the network in the past 24 hours, with long positions accounting for $570 million.
